desc: Ometepe rises from Lake Nicaragua as two volcanoes linked by a narrow isthmus, making it ideal for travelers who want low-key village life, jungle trails, and freshwater sunsets rather than fast-paced city breaks.
hls: Island formed by Concepción and Maderas volcanoes, with distinct hiking and scenery options.
Base areas like Moyogalpa and Balgüe suit slow itineraries with scooter or taxi day loops.
Dry season (roughly Dec–Apr) is typically easier for volcano trails and road conditions.
Petroglyphs, small museums, and community projects add cultural depth beyond nature time.
Excellent for cycling, kayaking, and lakefront viewpoints at a gentler daily pace.
Rainy months are greener and quieter but can bring muddy tracks and transport delays.

todo.name: Concepción volcano viewpoint hike
todo.desc: Take a guided early-start route to lower/mid-elevation viewpoints for lake panoramas; full summit attempts are strenuous and weather dependent.

todo.name: Maderas cloud-forest trail + San Ramón falls
todo.desc: Explore the wetter south side with forest paths and waterfall viewpoints, ideally with a local guide in rainy periods.

todo.name: Ojo de Agua swim stop
todo.desc: Plan a midday swim break at the spring-fed pools when temperatures are highest.

todo.name: Charco Verde + lakeshore sunset
todo.desc: Visit Charco Verde reserve for easy nature walks, then finish at a west-facing shoreline for sunset.

todo.name: Santo Domingo beach and village circuit
todo.desc: Combine beach downtime with roadside food stops and small village markets along the isthmus.

todo.name: Petroglyph and farm visit
todo.desc: Add one archaeology/agrotourism stop to learn about pre-Columbian rock carvings and island agriculture.

wifi.note: Reliable enough in better guesthouses around Moyogalpa/Balgüe; outages and slower evening speeds are common outside core hubs.

prac.fltNote: No UK nonstop; most itineraries route UK → Managua (MGA) via US/EU hubs, then continue by road to San Jorge and ferry to Ometepe. Return fares are often ~£520–£900 off-peak, ~£900–£1,250 in shoulder demand, and ~£1,250+ around Christmas/New Year and Easter peaks.
prac.currency: Nicaraguan córdoba (NIO); cash is important on Ometepe for ferries, motos, and smaller eateries.
prac.lang: Spanish is dominant; English is common in some eco-lodges/tour operators but inconsistent in smaller villages.
prac.tz: UTC-06:00 (Central Time, no DST in Nicaragua).
prac.visa: Check Nicaragua entry rules and any transit-visa requirements for your flight connection country before booking.
alerts: Ferry schedules can shift with weather and lake conditions—avoid tight same-day international flight connections.
Road surfaces vary; allow extra time if self-driving scooters/motorbikes in wet months.
Power cuts and ATM downtime happen periodically, so keep backup cash.

sweet: February to April is usually the easiest weather window for hiking and road mobility; November to January can balance greener scenery with manageable rain between holiday peaks.
